whatItMeasures: The WBC count measures the total number of white blood cells (leukocytes) in a given volume of blood; white blood cells are produced in the bone marrow and are the primary cells of the immune system, so an abnormal count can indicate infection, inflammation, immune disorders, or bone marrow problems.
causesOfHigh: Bacterial infection (most common cause of elevated WBC)
Viral infection (usually causes a more modest rise)
Leukaemia (abnormal overproduction of white blood cells)
Post-splenectomy (the spleen normally removes old WBCs)
causesOfLow: Viral infections (HIV, hepatitis, influenza — can suppress bone marrow)
Bone marrow disorders (aplastic anaemia, myelodysplastic syndrome)
Autoimmune conditions attacking white blood cells (lupus)
Overwhelming severe infection (sepsis can paradoxically deplete WBCs)

high: Easy bruising or bleeding (when leukaemia affects platelet production)
low: Increased susceptibility to infections — frequent, severe, or unusual infections

citations.title: Aplastic Anemia
citations.source: National Heart, Lung, and Blood Institute (NHLBI)
